title: armola deltoidea
intro: Armola deltoidea, also known as the 'Indian Spurge' or 'Euphorbia neriifolia', is a medicinal plant native to India and Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which are attributed to the presence of alkaloids and saponins. The health benefits of Armola deltoidea include the treatment of fever, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant is typically prepared as a decoction or a paste, often mixed with other herbs, and consumed orally to reap its medicinal benefits.
